# Offer Certainty Engine

Most ads fail because the offer is weak, not because the copy is weak.

An offer is the immediate exchange the buyer is asked to make.

## Offer ladder

| Offer | Buyer risk | Best use |
|---|---:|---|
| Read post | Very low | Cold education. |
| Watch demo | Low | Skeptical but curious buyer. |
| Use calculator | Low | Pain quantification. |
| Upload sample | Medium-low | Product-led proof. |
| Start free trial | Medium | Product has fast activation. |
| Book demo | Medium-high | Complex B2B buying. |
| Paid pilot | High | Enterprise or implementation-heavy. |
| Annual contract | Very high | Only after trust and proof. |

## Strong offers for software

- no-credit-card sandbox,
- sample-data demo,
- integration checker,
- migration estimate,
- ROI calculator,
- benchmark report,
- teardown/diagnostic,
- paid pilot credited toward annual plan,
- setup guarantee,
- proof-before-payment workflow.

## Offer strengthening questions

```text
Can the buyer experience proof before paying?
Can they test with fake/sample data?
Can they see exact pricing?
Can they cancel easily?
Can they export data?
Can they evaluate without a sales call?
Can they compare against their current workflow?
```

## Agent rule

Do not generate another ad until the offer is classified.

Ad quality cannot compensate for a bad offer at scale.
